design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling whip-based combat and platforming across exotic locations (tombs, trains, canyons) to collect three ancient idols
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Race against rival treasure hunter Brady Tresidder to locate world-destroying artifacts before they can be weaponized
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Archaeological adventurer traversing global locations with whip-based combat, inspired by pulp adventure fiction of the 1930s
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately emulates Castlevania's whip-combat template while differentiating through multi-sprite ragdoll-like character animation, limited-use secondary weapons tied to specific areas, and exotic international settings rather than gothic horror aesthetic

progression.upgrade_system: Secondary weapons (exploding rocks, morning star, hammer-like weapon) found in specific areas and used contextually within those zones
progression.unlock_mechanism: Geographic progression through sequential locations: Mexico tombs → Peru ruins → moving trains → forest → Grand Canyon
replayability_hooks: Multiple secondary weapons with limited application encouraging different playstyles per area
Companion character Annet Myer introduced mid-journey affects narrative framing

world_building_techniques: 1930s period setting establishing pulp adventure tone
H.P. Lovecraft-influenced mythology (Hastur cult reference) integrated into treasure-hunting premise
Environmental storytelling through exotic real-world locations (Peru ruins, Mexican tombs, Grand Canyon)
Animated cutscenes in Mega-CD version (by Madhouse studio) contrasting with Genesis text-based narrative delivery

notable_bosses: Stonehead enemies (depicted in promotional imagery using whip attacks)

faction_indicators: Earnest Evans III (protagonist) - artifact preservationist legacy
Brady Tresidder (rival) - destructive treasure hunter antagonist
Annet Myer (ally) - mysterious companion with unknown backstory
Sigfried (mysterious figure) - ambiguous intentions regarding Hastur cult
danger_signals: Three ancient idols capable of destroying Earth
Brady Tresidder's competitive acquisition race for apocalyptic artifacts
environmental_storytelling: Moving train sequences suggesting global pursuit
Forest and canyon environments representing natural barriers to treasure
